channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Critically appraised papers: Adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages does not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain [commentary].
item.description: A commentary indicates that for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain, adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages did not improve pain beyond home exercise alone.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Smallest worthwhile effect of anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction compared with non-surgical management: a benefit-harm trade-off study.
item.description: A benefit-harm trade-off study quantified the smallest worthwhile effect of ACL reconstruction versus non-surgical management, as perceived by 92 recreational or competitive athletes, for improving knee function and return to sport.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [synopsis].
item.description: A critically appraised paper concluded that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as, and health status more than, center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Recent highlights in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke.
item.description: A systematic review identified 118 RCTs, protocols, and systematic reviews with meta-analyses since 2020, highlighting recent advances in motor recovery and rehabilitation research after stroke and providing recommendations for future trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes
item.description: Exergaming offers multi-domain benefits for sarcopenia management in institutionalized older adults, encompassing physical, psychological, and social outcomes, aligning with a biopsychosocial understanding of the condition.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Innovative technologies for gait rehabilitation in incomplete spinal cord injury: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of studies from inception to March 2025 found innovative technological interventions effectively improve gait-related outcomes in adults with incomplete spinal cord injury.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Two-Year Outcomes of the Proximal Hamstring Avulsion Rehabilitation Regimes: Longitudinal Versus Accelerated Protocol (PHARRLAP) Study: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A prospective RCT (n=60) with 2-year follow-up found no significant differences in clinical outcomes, strength, or functional symmetry between conservative and accelerated rehabilitation protocols after proximal hamstring tendon repair.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy of High-Intensity Laser Therapy for Meralgia Paresthetica: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Sham-Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled trial (n=54 analyzed) found that High-Intensity Laser Therapy significantly improved pain (NPRS, P=0.006), function (LEFS, P=0.043), and lateral femoral cutaneous nerve sensory conduction velocity (P=0.027) in Meralgia Paresthetica patients at week 12 compared to sham.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: rTMS Over Dorsolateral Prefrontal Cortex Augments Dual-Task Training for Mobility, Balance and Cognition in Sub-Acute Stroke: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: An RCT found adding repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) over DLPFC to dual-task exercise significantly augmented dual-task mobility, balance, and cognitive performance in 30 sub-acute stroke patients.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
item.description: This prospective observational study found that neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing are common after stroke and are associated with poorer rehabilitation outcomes, impacting functional status and quality of life after a 4-week rehabilitation program.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Congress looks to drag healthcare private equity into the light
item.description: The U.S. Congress is seeking to increase transparency and scrutiny on private equity involvement in the healthcare sector.
item.author: Modern Healthcare

item.title: Comparative Effects of Land-Based and Aquatic Physiotherapy on Functional and Neuromuscular Outcomes in the Rigid-Akinetic Subtype of Parkinson's Disease: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
item.description: This randomized clinical trial involving 30 individuals with rigid-akinetic Parkinson's disease found that both 12 weeks of land-based and aquatic physiotherapy improved motor function, providing comparative effects on functional and neuromuscular outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of physical therapy techniques and methods in the management of endometriosis symptoms: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of various physical therapy methods and techniques in improving endometriosis symptoms, calculating mean differences and 95% confidence intervals.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Health Literacy During Inpatient Rehabilitation and Its Association with One-Year Health and Functional Outcomes in Individuals with Traumatic Spinal Cord Injury.
item.description: A cohort study (N=3,943) found that limited health literacy during inpatient rehabilitation for traumatic spinal cord injury was associated with poorer one-year health and functional outcomes, including higher rates of UTIs, pressure injuries, and rehospitalizations.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: A systematic review establishes weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury, addressing the high recurrence rate and lack of consensus.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 trials (n=1372) found shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]) and walking ability (mean -0.97; 95% CI [-1.30, -0.65]) for knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cognitive strategies for improving activity-level outcomes post stroke: an integrative review.
item.description: An integrative review identified 40 studies (half RCTs) on cognitive strategy interventions for post-stroke cognitive impairments, focusing on rehabilitation engagement and activity-level outcomes, with interventions delivered from 6 days to 9.5 years post-stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Read more about Calendar Year (CY) 2027 End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) Prospective Payment System Proposed Rule
item.description: CMS has released the proposed rule for the Calendar Year (CY) 2027 End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) Prospective Payment System, outlining potential changes to reimbursement and policies.
item.author: CMS

item.title: Incremental predictive value of spatiotemporal gait parameters beyond clinical measures for achieving independent outdoor ambulation in subacute stroke patients: A retrospective cohort study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that spatiotemporal gait parameters offer incremental predictive value beyond clinical measures for achieving independent outdoor ambulation in subacute stroke patients.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Accuracy of visual estimation of shoulder abduction by expert physiotherapists: a comparative study using 3D motion capture.
item.description: This cross-sectional study assessed the agreement between video-based expert visual estimation of shoulder abduction and 3D motion capture in healthy participants, highlighting accuracy considerations for clinical decision-making.
item.author: PubMed
